I, who had thought so well of myself and my abilities, of my capacity to surmount obstacles, was cornered at
last.
pg8-p1-s3: Now I was to plunge into the dark, joining that endless procession of sots who had gone on before.
pg8-p1-s4:
I thought of my
poor wife.
pg8-p1-s5: There had been much happiness after all.
pg8-p1-s6: What would I not give to make amends.
pg8-p1:
pride. I, who had thought so well of myself and my abilities, of my capacity to surmount obstacles, was cornered at
last. Now I was to plunge into the dark, joining that endless procession of sots who had gone on before. I thought of my
poor wife. There had been much happiness after all. What would I not give to make amends. But that was over now.
pg8-p2-s1: No words can tell of the loneliness and despair I found in that bitter morass of self-pity.
pg8-p2-s2:
Quicksand stretched around
me in all directions.
pg8-p2:
No words can tell of the loneliness and despair I found in that bitter morass of self-pity. Quicksand stretched around
me in all directions. I had met my match. I had been overwhelmed. Alcohol was my master.
pg8-p3-s1: Trembling, I stepped from the hospital a broken man.
pg8-p3-s2: Fear sobered me for a bit.
pg8-p3-s3:
Then came the insidious insanity of that
first drink, and on Armistice Day 1934, I was off again.
pg8-p3-s4:
Everyone became resigned to the certainty that I would have to
be shut up somewhere, or would stumble along to a miserable end.
pg8-p3-s5: How dark it is before the dawn!
pg8-p3-s6:
In reality that was the
beginning of my last debauch.
pg8-p3-s7: I was soon to be catapulted into what I like to call the fourth dimension of existence.
pg8-p3-s8:
I
was to know happiness, peace, and usefulness, in a way of life that is incredibly more wonderful as time passes.
pg8-p3:
Trembling, I stepped from the hospital a broken man. Fear sobered me for a bit. Then came the insidious insanity of that
first drink, and on Armistice Day 1934, I was off again. Everyone became resigned to the certainty that I would have to
be shut up somewhere, or would stumble along to a miserable end. How dark it is before the dawn! In reality that was the
beginning of my last debauch. I was soon to be catapulted into what I like to call the fourth dimension of existence. I
was to know happiness, peace, and usefulness, in a way of life that is incredibly more wonderful as time passes.
pg8-p4-s1: Near the end of that bleak November, I sat drinking in my kitchen.
pg8-p4-s2:
With a certain satisfaction I reflected there was
enough gin concealed about the house to carry me through that night and the next day.
pg8-p4-s4:
I wondered
whether I dared hide a full bottle of gin near the head of our bed.
pg8-p4-s5: I would need it before daylight.
pg8-p4:
Near the end of that bleak November, I sat drinking in my kitchen. With a certain satisfaction I reflected there was
enough gin concealed about the house to carry me through that night and the next day. My wife was at work. I wondered
whether I dared hide a full bottle of gin near the head of our bed. I would need it before daylight.
pg8-p5-s1: My musing was interrupted by the telephone.
pg8-p5-s2: The cheery voice of an old school friend asked if he might
pg8-p5: My musing was interrupted by the telephone. The cheery voice of an old school friend asked if he might
